title: Add Help Overlay Regression Tests
description: Extend TUI regression coverage in `tests/test_tui.py` to lock in help overlay toggle and close behavior, with assertions that the feature does not interfere with existing runtime state transitions relevant to refresh and merge flows.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify `?` toggles the help overlay on and off.
Tests verify `Esc` closes the help overlay.
Tests verify overlay close returns control to the underlying TUI state without breaking existing keybinding behavior.
Tests exercise behavior closely enough to catch regressions in refresh loop or merge confirmation state caused by the overlay implementation.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `tests/test_tui.py` with two regression tests: one verifies `Esc` closes the help overlay and restores the `r` refresh keybinding, and another verifies a pending merge survives the overlay, `Enter` is ignored while the overlay is open, and merge confirmation works after closing it. Ran `python -m unittest tests.test_tui` successfully (16 tests, OK).
handoff_summary.risks: Coverage remains focused on the TUI regression surface in this bead. It does not add broader end-to-end assertions outside `tests/test_tui.py`.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Tests rely on current help-overlay modal key handling and the default TUI status messages; downstream changes to those strings or bindings will require test updates.

conflict_risks: Tests rely on current help-overlay modal key handling and the default TUI status messages; downstream changes to those strings or bindings will require test updates.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B0038-review-corrective

Added TUI regression tests for help overlay close behavior and verified overlay interactions do not break refresh or merge flows.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run targeted checks and summarize failures clearly.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes only when strictly necessary to execute or stabilize the tests.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
